vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by the visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are either absent, dormant, or in a state of advanced decay.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss. No visible plant matter; appears to be bare soil.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss. No visible plant matter; appears to be bare soil.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent or entirely withered.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss. No visible plant matter.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface. No signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface. No signs of Mexican Mint.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present. Soil surface shows debris/eggshell fragments, but no Pothos foliage.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface. No signs of Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp-appearing across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of white, calcified fragments (likely crushed eggshells) concentrated in the P3/P4 area.
biome_observations.anomalies: A cylindrical object (likely a sensor or probe) is inserted into the yellow pot (P1) and remains static throughout the sequence.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: The environment remains static. No growth, no movement, no emergence of biological life.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No change. The biome is in a state of total stasis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of active photosynthesis or vegetative growth. The presence of the white rabbit anchor suggests a controlled environment, but the lack of plant matter indicates a total failure of the botanical specimens.
anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells as a soil amendment/top dressing.
Insertion of a sensor probe in P1.
Total absence of expected plant life despite the presence of pots and soil.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline of the pots and their expected contents. Upon validation, it is clear that the registry does not match the physical reality of the desk. The pots are present, but they are devoid of the specified plants. The environment appears to be a 'dead' biome, characterized by bare soil and inorganic debris (eggshells). The white rabbit anchor is the only consistent feature, confirming the camera's focus, but the botanical subjects have suffered a systemic loss.
